title: Project - transcribe.cpp
excerpt: A place to play around with technology and make it easy and fun
content_html:
I'm super excited to share transcribe.cpp today.

transcribe.cpp is a ggml based transcription library which supports all the latest transcription models. Every model published under the handy-computer HF org has been numerically validated and WER tested to match the reference implementation. It's accelerated everywhere.

I'm the author and maintainer of Handy. This library grew from the pains of distributing a cross-platform speech-to-text application to many people.

This is a v0.1.0 library which means that there are some rough edges which I cannot discover alone! Please report them, and let's fix them together!

Motivation

Let me say this. I think distributing a cross-platform application with the current ASR inference stack is terrible.

You've basically got whisper.cpp and ONNX. That's it. You could roll MLX in for Apple devices, but now you've to support two different engines and port models to each. I've been a fan of ONNX for getting model support into Handy quickly, but so much performance is left on the table with CPU only.

There are a few random libraries out there which claim to support a lot of models, but they have unknown authors, and unknown testing, as far as I've seen. They leave me with more questions than answers.

When will they stop maintaining this library? Has the creator thought about bindings so you can actually use it in a real desktop or mobile app? Is this effectively demo code? Have they benchmarked it? Is it faster than ONNX?

And this is what led to transcribe.cpp. As Handy's maintainer I needed a library I could trust. Where I could download a file and run inference on it. Where I can know that the inference coming from the model in the engine is as good as the reference implementation. The inference should run on the GPU for the best performance. It should be trivially embeddable in Handy, it cannot be a huge pytorch lib. It must be something that works on Mac, Windows, and Linux. And ggml seemed like by far the best way forward. It has a strong community, and a great distribution story.

So what do you get?

You get a fast and accurate inference engine with wide ranging model support.

  • Support for 16 ASR Families (60+ models) with more coming
  • Acceleration via Vulkan, Metal, CUDA, and TinyBLAS
  • Every model has been numerically verified and WER tested
  • Support for Streaming Transcription
  • Support for Batch Transcription
  • More or less drop in whisper.cpp replacement
  • Maintainer supported bindings in 4 Languages
    • Python
    • Javascript/Typescript
    • Rust
    • ObjC/Swift

Wide Model Support

We intend to support as many state-of-the-art transcription models as possible. As of today, we support most of the modern transcription models that are publicly available. There are a few missing still, but they will be added soon.

Acceleration Support

One of my top goals was to run any ASR model I wanted on Vulkan. In my opinion this is the floor for any application shipping local inference. For every model we support, there is a corresponding benchmark run from a Ryzen 4750U (CPU + Vulkan) on Fedora as well as on my M4 Max.

Numerically Verified

I also wanted to make sure that inference in transcribe.cpp is accurate and as close to the reference implementation as possible. This largely came from a huge degree of uncertainty of inference accuracy when using .onnx models I found on Hugging Face. In order to ensure the inference we do is correct we numerically validate every model versus the reference. On top of numerical validation, we run full WER sweeps to make sure that whatever the reference is outputting, we output the same thing. That means every model has run through thousands of utterances and is very close or same as the reference. And the results of this data are published in the transcribe.cpp repo as well as with each model on Hugging Face.

Drop In whisper.cpp replacement

transcribe.cpp is more or less a drop in support for whisper.cpp. The main reason for this is: Handy used whisper.cpp and I needed to ship an update with transcribe.cpp which would replace it. I needed to keep some compatibility with the very popular .bin files which run in whisper.cpp and shipped with Handy. transcribe.cpp can run them. There are some flags and features in whisper.cpp which we do not support yet. But I think for the vast majority of use cases our whisper implementation is solid and can replace whisper.cpp while having about equal performance.

Real Distribution

Language bindings were on my mind to begin with. While this library is written in C/C++, I needed bindings in Rust. And I also knew that in order for us to distribute local transcription as widely as possible, it requires at minimum decent first-party support of bindings. I've chosen 4 languages that I think are fairly representative of where people will use the library. I welcome others to contribute bindings directly to the project as well, assuming that they are willing to take on the maintenance burden of doing so.

And of course, at the end of the day, a lot of the decisions were driven by Handy. As a result of Handy being popular, I intend to maintain this library, just as I've done my best to maintain Handy. I intend to be someone who continues to maintain open source projects and contribute to the ecosystem where I can.

This library never would have existed without Handy because I wouldn't have had the problem of trying to support a bunch of different ASR models. I would have never learned all the use cases that people have for ASR. I've done my best to cover the ones that I hear about the most. Certainly, there are cases in the library that are not currently handled. If there are things that I missed, you are free to contribute to the library!

Making Local Speech to Text More Accessible

transcribe.cpp is aimed squarely at making locally run ASR easier. We know that transcription can run extremely accurately on most devices, and there should be no need to send your voice to a cloud service. An RK3566 can run models via transcribe.cpp faster than real time on its anemic CPU. Faster than real time transcription with SOTA models runs in a handful of watts. It's not a hope or a dream, it's a fact.

I think as we look forward to the future, more inference will start happening locally for one reason or the other. This brings the distribution story front and center. In order to have more applications running inference locally, we need to make running inference easier. Certainly transcribe.cpp does not solve this on the whole, and there is a long way to go, but I hope it's a small step forward. I've certainly learned a lot.

Gratitude

I am extremely thankful for all the folks who have supported this project.

First and foremost is to Mozilla AI, their BiR program, and Davide from Mozilla AI. This project was largely a problem in my head that I came to them with, and they decided to support me in solving the problem. At the time transcribe.cpp wasn't even a concrete idea, I was just exploring how to solve accelerated distribution in Handy. So a huge thanks to them, their support, and helping to bring this project into existence.

ggml. This project wouldn't be possible without ggml and all of the contributors to it. Thank you all so much for the work you've done. I think ggml really does amazing work in helping to make distributing local inference applications easy and possible.

Modal has also been a critical help for me. I reached out to them, and they gave me credits. These credits are put towards doing the WER testing and ensuring the library works well on CUDA. It is an immense help being able to verify the correctness of the work.

Blacksmith helps to power some of the CI/CD for transcribe.cpp. Again I reached out to them and they immediately responded with credits. Of course CI/CD is critical for making sure everything put out has been tested to at least some degree.

Hugging Face both for being a pillar in the local AI community, as well as providing the handy-computer org private storage, so I could upload models at my own will.

AI Assisted?

Yes absolutely. I don't think it's possible for a single individual to write an engine from scratch of this size using ggml in a handful of months without outside assistance. Were any of the words here written using AI? Nope. They came from my mouth or my fingers.

blocks.html_en: Numerically Verified

sentences.en: Numerically Verified
sentences.zh: 数值验证

blocks.html_en: I also wanted to make sure that inference in transcribe.cpp is accurate and as close to the reference implementation as possible. This largely came from a huge degree of uncertainty of inference accuracy when using .onnx models I found on Hugging Face. In order to ensure the inference we do is correct we numerically validate every model versus the reference. That means every model has run through thousands of utterances and is very close or same as the reference. And the results of this data are published in the transcribe.cpp repo as well as with each model on Hugging Face.

sentences.en: I also wanted to make sure that inference in transcribe.cpp is accurate and as close to the reference implementation as possible.
sentences.zh: 我还想确保 transcribe.cpp 中的推理是准确的并且尽可能接近参考实现。

sentences.en: This largely came from a huge degree of uncertainty of inference accuracy when using .onnx models I found on Hugging Face.
sentences.zh: 这很大程度上来自于使用我在 Hugging Face 上发现的 .onnx 模型时推理准确性的巨大不确定性。

sentences.en: In order to ensure the inference we do is correct we numerically validate every model versus the reference.
sentences.zh: 为了确保我们所做的推论是正确的，我们对每个模型与参考进行数值验证。

sentences.en: On top of numerical validation, we run full WER sweeps to make sure that whatever the reference is outputting, we output the same thing.
sentences.zh: 除了数值验证之外，我们还运行完整的 WER 扫描，以确保无论参考输出如何，我们都会输出相同的内容。

sentences.en: That means every model has run through thousands of utterances and is very close or same as the reference.
sentences.zh: 这意味着每个模型都经过了数千次表达，并且与参考非常接近或相同。

sentences.en: And the results of this data are published in the transcribe.cpp repo as well as with each model on Hugging Face.
sentences.zh: 这些数据的结果发布在 transcribe.cpp 存储库以及 Hugging Face 上的每个模型中。

blocks.html_en: Drop In whisper.cpp replacement

sentences.en: Drop In whisper.cpp replacement
sentences.zh: 直接替换 耳语.cpp

blocks.html_en: transcribe.cpp is more or less a drop in support for whisper.cpp. The main reason for this is: Handy used whisper.cpp and I needed to ship an update with transcribe.cpp which would replace it. I needed to keep some compatibility with the very popular .bin files which run in whisper.cpp and shipped with Handy. transcribe.cpp can run them. There are some flags and features in whisper.cpp which we do not support yet. But I think for the vast majority of use cases our whisper implementation is solid and can replace whisper.cpp while having about equal performance.

sentences.en: transcribe.cpp is more or less a drop in support for whisper.cpp.
sentences.zh: transcribe.cpp 或多或少地减少了对whisper.cpp 的支持。

sentences.en: The main reason for this is: Handy used whisper.cpp and I needed to ship an update with transcribe.cpp which would replace it.
sentences.zh: 主要原因是：Handy 使用了 Whisper.cpp，我需要使用 transcribe.cpp 发布更新来替换它。

sentences.en: I needed to keep some compatibility with the very popular .bin files which run in whisper.cpp and shipped with Handy.
sentences.zh: 我需要与非常流行的 .bin 文件保持一定的兼容性，这些文件在 Whisper.cpp 中运行并随 Handy 一起提供。

sentences.en: transcribe.cpp can run them.
sentences.zh: transcribe.cpp 可以运行它们。

sentences.en: There are some flags and features in whisper.cpp which we do not support yet.
sentences.zh: 耳语.cpp 中有一些我们尚不支持的标志和功能。

sentences.en: But I think for the vast majority of use cases our whisper implementation is solid and can replace whisper.cpp while having about equal performance.
sentences.zh: 但我认为对于绝大多数用例，我们的耳语实现是可靠的，可以取代耳语.cpp，同时具有大致相同的性能。

blocks.html_en: Real Distribution

sentences.en: Real Distribution
sentences.zh: 实际分布

blocks.html_en: Language bindings were on my mind to begin with. While this library is written in C/C++, I needed bindings in Rust. And I also knew that in order for us to distribute local transcription as widely as possible, it requires at minimum decent first-party support of bindings. I've chosen 4 languages that I think are fairly representative of where people will use the library. I welcome others to contribute bindings directly to the project as well, assuming that they are willing to take on the maintenance burden of doing so.

sentences.en: Language bindings were on my mind to begin with.
sentences.zh: 我一开始就想到了语言的束缚。

sentences.en: While this library is written in C/C++, I needed bindings in Rust.
sentences.zh: 虽然这个库是用 C/C++ 编写的，但我需要 Rust 中的绑定。

sentences.en: And I also knew that in order for us to distribute local transcription as widely as possible, it requires at minimum decent first-party support of bindings.
sentences.zh: 我还知道，为了让我们尽可能广泛地分发本地转录，它至少需要良好的第一方绑定支持。

sentences.en: I've chosen 4 languages that I think are fairly representative of where people will use the library.
sentences.zh: 我选择了 4 种我认为相当能代表人们使用该库的语言。

sentences.en: I welcome others to contribute bindings directly to the project as well, assuming that they are willing to take on the maintenance burden of doing so.
sentences.zh: 我也欢迎其他人直接为该项目贡献绑定，前提是他们愿意承担这样做的维护负担。

blocks.html_en: And of course, at the end of the day, a lot of the decisions were driven by Handy. As a result of Handy being popular, I intend to maintain this library, just as I've done my best to maintain Handy. I intend to be someone who continues to maintain open source projects and contribute to the ecosystem where I can.

sentences.en: And of course, at the end of the day, a lot of the decisions were driven by Handy.
sentences.zh: 当然，归根结底，很多决定都是由 Handy 推动的。

sentences.en: As a result of Handy being popular, I intend to maintain this library, just as I've done my best to maintain Handy.
sentences.zh: 由于 Handy 很受欢迎，我打算维护这个库，就像我尽力维护 Handy 一样。

sentences.en: I intend to be someone who continues to maintain open source projects and contribute to the ecosystem where I can.
sentences.zh: 我打算成为一个继续维护开源项目并尽我所能为生态系统做出贡献的人。

blocks.html_en: This library never would have existed without Handy because I wouldn't have had the problem of trying to support a bunch of different ASR models. I would have never learned all the use cases that people have for ASR. I've done my best to cover the ones that I hear about the most. Certainly, there are cases in the library that are not currently handled. If there are things that I missed, you are free to contribute to the library!

sentences.en: This library never would have existed without Handy because I wouldn't have had the problem of trying to support a bunch of different ASR models.
sentences.zh: 如果没有 Handy，这个库永远不会存在，因为我不会遇到尝试支持一堆不同 ASR 模型的问题。

sentences.en: I would have never learned all the use cases that people have for ASR.
sentences.zh: 我永远不会了解人们对 ASR 的所有用例。

sentences.en: I've done my best to cover the ones that I hear about the most.
sentences.zh: 我已尽力报道我最常听到的内容。

sentences.en: Certainly, there are cases in the library that are not currently handled.
sentences.zh: 当然，图书馆里还有一些案件目前还没有处理。

sentences.en: If there are things that I missed, you are free to contribute to the library!
sentences.zh: 如果我错过了一些东西，您可以自由地为图书馆做出贡献！

blocks.html_en: Making Local Speech to Text More Accessible

sentences.en: Making Local Speech to Text More Accessible
sentences.zh: 让本地语音转文本更方便

blocks.html_en: transcribe.cpp is aimed squarely at making locally run ASR easier. We know that transcription can run extremely accurately on most devices, and there should be no need to send your voice to a cloud service. An RK3566 can run models via transcribe.cpp faster than real time on its anemic CPU. Faster than real time transcription with SOTA models runs in a handful of watts. It's not a hope or a dream, it's a fact.

sentences.en: transcribe.cpp is aimed squarely at making locally run ASR easier.
sentences.zh: transcribe.cpp 的直接目标是使本地运行的 ASR 更容易。

sentences.en: We know that transcription can run extremely accurately on most devices, and there should be no need to send your voice to a cloud service.
sentences.zh: 我们知道转录可以在大多数设备上极其准确地运行，并且不需要将您的语音发送到云服务。

sentences.en: An RK3566 can run models via transcribe.cpp faster than real time on its anemic CPU.
sentences.zh: RK3566 可以通过 transcribe.cpp 在其贫乏的 CPU 上比实时更快地运行模型。

sentences.en: Faster than real time transcription with SOTA models runs in a handful of watts.
sentences.zh: SOTA 模型的转录速度比实时转录更快，功耗仅为几瓦。

sentences.en: It's not a hope or a dream, it's a fact.
sentences.zh: 这不是希望或梦想，而是事实。

blocks.html_en: I think as we look forward to the future, more inference will start happening locally for one reason or the other. This brings the distribution story front and center. In order to have more applications running inference locally, we need to make running inference easier. Certainly transcribe.cpp does not solve this on the whole, and there is a long way to go, but I hope it's a small step forward. I've certainly learned a lot.

sentences.en: I think as we look forward to the future, more inference will start happening locally for one reason or the other.
sentences.zh: 我认为，当我们展望未来时，出于某种原因，更多的推理将开始在本地发生。

sentences.en: This brings the distribution story front and center.
sentences.zh: 这使得发行故事成为焦点。

sentences.en: In order to have more applications running inference locally, we need to make running inference easier.
sentences.zh: 为了让更多的应用程序在本地运行推理，我们需要使运行推理变得更容易。

sentences.en: Certainly transcribe.cpp does not solve this on the whole, and there is a long way to go, but I hope it's a small step forward.
sentences.zh: 当然 transcribe.cpp 并没有从整体上解决这个问题，还有很长的路要走，但我希望这是向前迈出的一小步。

sentences.en: I've certainly learned a lot.
sentences.zh: 我当然学到了很多东西。

blocks.html_en: Gratitude

sentences.en: Gratitude
sentences.zh: 感激

blocks.html_en: I am extremely thankful for all the folks who have supported this project.

sentences.en: I am extremely thankful for all the folks who have supported this project.
sentences.zh: 我非常感谢所有支持这个项目的人们。

blocks.html_en: First and foremost is to Mozilla AI, their BiR program, and Davide from Mozilla AI. This project was largely a problem in my head that I came to them with, and they decided to support me in solving the problem. At the time transcribe.cpp wasn't even a concrete idea, I was just exploring how to solve accelerated distribution in Handy. So a huge thanks to them, their support, and helping to bring this project into existence.

sentences.en: First and foremost is to Mozilla AI, their BiR program, and Davide from Mozilla AI.
sentences.zh: 首先也是最重要的是 Mozilla AI、他们的 BiR 程序以及来自 Mozilla AI 的 Davide。

sentences.en: This project was largely a problem in my head that I came to them with, and they decided to support me in solving the problem.
sentences.zh: 这个项目很大程度上是我向他们提出的一个问题，他们决定支持我解决这个问题。

sentences.en: At the time transcribe.cpp wasn't even a concrete idea, I was just exploring how to solve accelerated distribution in Handy.
sentences.zh: 当时transcribe.cpp还不是一个具体的想法，我只是在探索如何解决Handy中的加速分发问题。

sentences.en: So a huge thanks to them, their support, and helping to bring this project into existence.
sentences.zh: 非常感谢他们、他们的支持以及帮助使这个项目得以实现。

blocks.html_en: ggml. This project wouldn't be possible without ggml and all of the contributors to it. Thank you all so much for the work you've done. I think ggml really does amazing work in helping to make distributing local inference applications easy and possible.

sentences.en: ggml.
sentences.zh: ggml。

sentences.en: This project wouldn't be possible without ggml and all of the contributors to it.
sentences.zh: 如果没有 ggml 及其所有贡献者，这个项目就不可能实现。

sentences.en: Thank you all so much for the work you've done.
sentences.zh: 非常感谢大家所做的工作。

sentences.en: I think ggml really does amazing work in helping to make distributing local inference applications easy and possible.
sentences.zh: 我认为 ggml 在帮助轻松且可能地分发本地推理应用程序方面确实做了令人惊叹的工作。

blocks.html_en: Modal has also been a critical help for me. I reached out to them, and they gave me credits. These credits are put towards doing the WER testing and ensuring the library works well on CUDA. It is an immense help being able to verify the correctness of the work.

sentences.en: Modal has also been a critical help for me.
sentences.zh: 莫代尔对我来说也提供了重要的帮助。

sentences.en: I reached out to them, and they gave me credits.
sentences.zh: 我联系了他们，他们给了我学分。

sentences.en: These credits are put towards doing the WER testing and ensuring the library works well on CUDA.
sentences.zh: 这些积分用于进行 WER 测试并确保该库在 CUDA 上运行良好。

sentences.en: It is an immense help being able to verify the correctness of the work.
sentences.zh: 能够验证工作的正确性是一个巨大的帮助。

blocks.html_en: Blacksmith helps to power some of the CI/CD for transcribe.cpp. Again I reached out to them and they immediately responded with credits. Of course CI/CD is critical for making sure everything put out has been tested to at least some degree.

sentences.en: Blacksmith helps to power some of the CI/CD for transcribe.cpp.
sentences.zh: Blacksmith 帮助为 transcribe.cpp 的一些 CI/CD 提供支持。

sentences.en: Again I reached out to them and they immediately responded with credits.
sentences.zh: 我再次联系他们，他们立即回复了积分。

sentences.en: Of course CI/CD is critical for making sure everything put out has been tested to at least some degree.
sentences.zh: 当然，CI/CD 对于确保发布的所有内容至少在某种程度上都经过测试至关重要。

blocks.html_en: Hugging Face both for being a pillar in the local AI community, as well as providing the handy-computer org private storage, so I could upload models at my own will.

sentences.en: Hugging Face both for being a pillar in the local AI community, as well as providing the handy-computer org private storage, so I could upload models at my own will.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 既是当地人工智能社区的支柱，又提供了便携式计算机组织的私人存储，这样我就可以随意上传模型。

blocks.html_en: AI Assisted?

sentences.en: AI Assisted?
sentences.zh: 人工智能辅助？

blocks.html_en: Yes absolutely. I don't think it's possible for a single individual to write an engine from scratch of this size using ggml in a handful of months without outside assistance. Were any of the words here written using AI? Nope. They came from my mouth or my fingers.

sentences.en: Yes absolutely.
sentences.zh: 是的，绝对是。

sentences.en: I don't think it's possible for a single individual to write an engine from scratch of this size using ggml in a handful of months without outside assistance.
sentences.zh: 我认为，在没有外部帮助的情况下，一个人不可能在几个月内使用 ggml 从头开始​​编写如此大小的引擎。

sentences.en: Were any of the words here written using AI?
sentences.zh: 这里的文字是用人工智能写的吗？

sentences.en: Nope.
sentences.zh: 没有。

sentences.en: They came from my mouth or my fingers.
sentences.zh: 它们来自我的嘴或我的手指。

content_html_zh:
我非常高兴今天能分享 transcribe.cpp。

transcribe.cpp 是一个基于 ggml 的转录库，支持所有最新的转录模型。 在手持计算机 HF 组织下发布的每个模型都经过数值验证和 WER 测试，以匹配参考实现。 到处都在加速。

我是 Handy 的作者和维护者。 这个库是在将跨平台语音到文本应用程序分发给许多人的痛苦中诞生的。

这是一个 v0.1.0 库，这意味着有一些我无法独自发现的粗糙边缘！ 请报告它们，让我们一起修复它们！

动机

让我这么说吧。 我认为使用当前的 ASR 推理堆栈分发跨平台应用程序非常糟糕。

您基本上已经有了 Whisper.cpp 和 ONNX。 就是这样。 您可以为 Apple 设备推出 MLX，但现在您必须支持两种不同的引擎和每个引擎的端口模型。 我一直是 ONNX 的粉丝，因为它可以快速将模型支持引入 Handy，但仅靠 CPU 就无法实现太多性能。

有一些随机库声称支持很多模型，但据我所知，它们的作者和测试都是未知的。 他们留给我的问题多于答案。

他们什么时候停止维护这个库？ 创建者是否考虑过绑定，以便您可以在真正的桌面或移动应用程序中实际使用它？ 这是有效的演示代码吗？ 他们对它进行了基准测试吗？ 它比 ONNX 更快吗？

这就是导致 transcribe.cpp 的原因。 作为 Handy 的维护者，我需要一个可以信任的库。 我可以在哪里下载文件并对其运行推理。 我可以知道来自引擎中模型的推断与参考实现一样好。 推理应在 GPU 上运行以获得最佳性能。 它应该可以简单地嵌入到 Handy 中，它不能是一个巨大的 pytorch 库。 它必须能够在 Mac、Windows 和 Linux 上运行。 ggml 似乎是迄今为止最好的前进方式。 它拥有强大的社区和精彩的发行故事。

那么你得到了什么？

您将获得快速、准确的推理引擎以及广泛的模型支持。

  • 支持 16 个 ASR 系列（60 多个型号），未来还会有更多型号
  • 通过 Vulkan、Metal、CUDA 和 TinyBLAS 加速
  • 每个模型都经过数值验证和 WER 测试
  • 支持流式转录
  • 支持批量转录
  • 或多或少地减少了 Whisper.cpp 的替换
  • 维护者支持 4 种语言的绑定 Python Javascript/Typescript Rust ObjC/Swift

广泛的模型支持

我们打算支持尽可能多的最先进的转录模型。 截至今天，我们支持大多数公开可用的现代转录模型。 还有一些缺失，但很快就会添加。

加速支持

我的首要目标之一是在 Vulkan 上运行我想要的任何 ASR 模型。 在我看来，这是任何传送本地推理的应用程序的底线。 对于我们支持的每种型号，在 Fedora 以及我的 M4 Max 上都有一个在 Ryzen 4750U（CPU + Vulkan）上运行的相应基准测试。

数值验证

我还想确保 transcribe.cpp 中的推理是准确的并且尽可能接近参考实现。 这很大程度上来自于使用我在 Hugging Face 上发现的 .onnx 模型时推理准确性的巨大不确定性。 为了确保我们所做的推论是正确的，我们对每个模型与参考进行数值验证。 除了数值验证之外，我们还运行完整的 WER 扫描，以确保无论参考输出如何，我们都会输出相同的内容。 这意味着每个模型都经过了数千次表达，并且与参考非常接近或相同。 这些数据的结果发布在 transcribe.cpp 存储库以及 Hugging Face 上的每个模型中。

直接替换 耳语.cpp

transcribe.cpp 或多或少地减少了对whisper.cpp 的支持。 主要原因是：Handy 使用了 Whisper.cpp，我需要使用 transcribe.cpp 发布更新来替换它。 我需要与非常流行的 .bin 文件保持一定的兼容性，这些文件在 Whisper.cpp 中运行并随 Handy 一起提供。 transcribe.cpp 可以运行它们。 耳语.cpp 中有一些我们尚不支持的标志和功能。 但我认为对于绝大多数用例，我们的耳语实现是可靠的，可以取代耳语.cpp，同时具有大致相同的性能。

实际分布

我一开始就想到了语言的束缚。 虽然这个库是用 C/C++ 编写的，但我需要 Rust 中的绑定。 我还知道，为了让我们尽可能广泛地分发本地转录，它至少需要良好的第一方绑定支持。 我选择了 4 种我认为相当能代表人们使用该库的语言。 我也欢迎其他人直接为该项目贡献绑定，前提是他们愿意承担这样做的维护负担。

当然，归根结底，很多决定都是由 Handy 推动的。 由于 Handy 很受欢迎，我打算维护这个库，就像我尽力维护 Handy 一样。 我打算成为一个继续维护开源项目并尽我所能为生态系统做出贡献的人。

如果没有 Handy，这个库永远不会存在，因为我不会遇到尝试支持一堆不同 ASR 模型的问题。 我永远不会了解人们对 ASR 的所有用例。 我已尽力报道我最常听到的内容。 当然，图书馆里还有一些案件目前还没有处理。 如果我错过了一些东西，您可以自由地为图书馆做出贡献！

让本地语音转文本更方便

transcribe.cpp 的直接目标是使本地运行的 ASR 更容易。 我们知道转录可以在大多数设备上极其准确地运行，并且不需要将您的语音发送到云服务。 RK3566 可以通过 transcribe.cpp 在其贫乏的 CPU 上比实时更快地运行模型。 SOTA 模型的转录速度比实时转录更快，功耗仅为几瓦。 这不是希望或梦想，而是事实。

我认为，当我们展望未来时，出于某种原因，更多的推理将开始在本地发生。 这使得发行故事成为焦点。 为了让更多的应用程序在本地运行推理，我们需要使运行推理变得更容易。 当然 transcribe.cpp 并没有从整体上解决这个问题，还有很长的路要走，但我希望这是向前迈出的一小步。 我当然学到了很多东西。

感激

我非常感谢所有支持这个项目的人们。

首先也是最重要的是 Mozilla AI、他们的 BiR 程序以及来自 Mozilla AI 的 Davide。 这个项目很大程度上是我向他们提出的一个问题，他们决定支持我解决这个问题。 当时transcribe.cpp还不是一个具体的想法，我只是在探索如何解决Handy中的加速分发问题。 非常感谢他们、他们的支持以及帮助使这个项目得以实现。

ggml。 如果没有 ggml 及其所有贡献者，这个项目就不可能实现。 非常感谢大家所做的工作。 我认为 ggml 在帮助轻松且可能地分发本地推理应用程序方面确实做了令人惊叹的工作。

莫代尔对我来说也提供了重要的帮助。 我联系了他们，他们给了我学分。 这些积分用于进行 WER 测试并确保该库在 CUDA 上运行良好。 能够验证工作的正确性是一个巨大的帮助。

Blacksmith 帮助为 transcribe.cpp 的一些 CI/CD 提供支持。 我再次联系他们，他们立即回复了积分。 当然，CI/CD 对于确保发布的所有内容至少在某种程度上都经过测试至关重要。

Hugging Face 既是当地人工智能社区的支柱，又提供了便携式计算机组织的私人存储，这样我就可以随意上传模型。

人工智能辅助？

是的，绝对是。 我认为，在没有外部帮助的情况下，一个人不可能在几个月内使用 ggml 从头开始​​编写如此大小的引擎。 这里的文字是用人工智能写的吗？ 没有。 它们来自我的嘴或我的手指。
